title: San Diego
summary: A relaxed coastal weekend in San Diego mixing iconic attractions, oceanfront walks, and water sports. Highlights include La Jolla Cove, Balboa Park, and sunsets over the Pacific.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Arrival & city
itinerary.text: Evening arrival into San Diego. Settle in and explore.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Coast & Oceanside
itinerary.text: Beach and coastal time, heading up toward Oceanside.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Breakfast & departure
itinerary.text: Relaxed breakfast before an afternoon flight home.

todo: San Diego Zoo — world-famous zoo in Balboa Park
La Jolla Cove — stunning views, seals, and snorkeling; combine with La Jolla Shores
Sunset Cliffs Natural Park — sunset views; a sea cave is accessible at low tide
Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve — coastal bluffs and sunset spot
Balboa Park — gardens, museums, and architecture
Seaport Village & Embarcadero — waterfront stroll to Tuna Harbor Park
Stand-up paddleboarding — rentals at Shelter Island's calm waters
Wakeboarding & water skiing charters
Encinitas Meditation Garden — peaceful ocean-view gardens
stay: Oceanside — coastal town north of the city
tips: Time a visit to the Sunset Cliffs sea cave for low tide.
Pair La Jolla Cove with nearby La Jolla Shores for beach time.

title: Everest Base Camp Trek
summary: A classic 12-day trek through Nepal's Khumbu region to Everest Base Camp (5,364m) and Kala Patthar (5,550m), with a scenic helicopter return. The route passes Sherpa villages, monasteries, and dramatic Himalayan peaks.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Arrive in Kathmandu (1,345m)
itinerary.text: Transfer to hotel, trek briefing, and final gear shopping in the city.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Fly to Lukla (2,840m) & trek to Phakding
itinerary.text: Scenic flight to Lukla, then a gradual descent along the Dudh Koshi River, crossing suspension bridges.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Trek to Namche Bazaar (3,440m)
itinerary.text: Through pine forest past Monjo (Sagarmatha NP entrance) and Jorsale, with a steep climb to Namche and first Everest views.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Acclimatization: Everest View Hotel
itinerary.text: Hike to the Everest View Hotel for panoramas of Ama Dablam, Thamserku, and Everest, plus the Everest Museum.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Trek to Deboche (3,860m)
itinerary.text: Climb to Tengboche to visit its iconic monastery, then continue to peaceful Deboche.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Trek to Dingboche (4,430m)
itinerary.text: Pass through Pangboche into high-altitude terrain with stone-walled fields.

itinerary.label: Day 7
itinerary.heading: Acclimatization hike to Nangkartshang (5,066m)
itinerary.text: A climb to a ridge with views of Makalu, Lhotse, and Ama Dablam, then back to Dingboche.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.heading: Trek to Lobuche (4,910m)
itinerary.text: Ascend the lateral moraine of the Khumbu Glacier with views of the Khumbu Icefall.

itinerary.label: Day 9
itinerary.heading: Everest Base Camp (5,364m)
itinerary.text: Trek across glacial moraine to Base Camp, then return to Gorakshep (5,164m) for the night.

itinerary.label: Day 10
itinerary.heading: Kala Patthar (5,550m) & descent
itinerary.text: Pre-dawn climb to Kala Patthar for sunrise over Everest, Lhotse, and Nuptse, then descend to Pheriche.

itinerary.label: Day 11
itinerary.heading: Return to Kathmandu
itinerary.text: Helicopter return option and Lukla–Kathmandu flight.

itinerary.label: Day 12
itinerary.heading: Departure
itinerary.text: Depart Kathmandu.

todo: Everest Base Camp (5,364m) — the trek's main destination
Kala Patthar (5,550m) — best sunrise viewpoint over Everest
Tengboche Monastery — the region's most important religious site
Everest View Hotel — ridge-top panoramas during acclimatization
Namche Bazaar — vibrant Sherpa hub town
Sagarmatha National Park — entered at Monjo
stay: Hotel in Kathmandu (start and end of trip)
Teahouses/lodges along the trail (Phakding, Namche, Deboche, Dingboche, Lobuche, Gorakshep, Pheriche)
tips: Build in acclimatization days (Namche and Dingboche) to reduce altitude-sickness risk.
Carry USD for the helicopter return and rupees for the road.
Down jackets and sleeping bags can be rented in Kathmandu or Namche.
Get a local SIM and insurance that covers high-altitude trekking and evacuation.
Pack layers: thermals, merino base layers, fleece, and a waterproof shell.

title: Hawaii: Big Island & Maui
summary: An eight-day island-hopping trip splitting time between the Big Island and Maui — volcanoes, waterfalls, black- and green-sand beaches, snorkeling, and the Road to Hana.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Big Island: Volcanoes NP
itinerary.text: Explore Hawaii Volcanoes National Park — Keanakako'i overlook, Thurston Lava Tube, Kilauea Iki, and Chain of Craters Road.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Waterfalls, surf & stargazing
itinerary.text: Waipi'o Valley lookout, Akaka Falls, an afternoon surf lesson in Kona, and sunset/stargazing on Mauna Kea.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Caves, beaches & manta rays
itinerary.text: Kaumana Caves, Rainbow Falls, Punalu'u black-sand and Papakolea green-sand beaches, then a nighttime manta-ray snorkel off Kona.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Historic park & snorkeling
itinerary.text: Pu'uhonua o Honaunau historic park and snorkeling at Two Step, then sunset at the beach.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Maui: Road to Hana
itinerary.text: Morning flight to Maui. Drive the Road to Hana halfway — Ho'okipa Beach, swim at Twin Falls.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Haleakala & west coast
itinerary.text: Haleakala sunrise (book ahead), Maui Ocean Center, the Nakalele Blowhole, and snorkeling at Black Rock Beach.

itinerary.label: Day 7
itinerary.heading: Molokini & beaches
itinerary.text: Molokini Crater snorkel tour from Maalaea Harbor, then a relaxed sunset at Ulua Beach.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.heading: Departure
itinerary.text: Morning at Baby Beach before an early afternoon flight home.

food: Lava Lava Beach Club (Big Island) — beachfront dinner
Mika (Maui) — dinner on the Road to Hana
todo: Hawaii Volcanoes National Park — craters, lava tube, Chain of Craters Road
Mauna Kea — visitor center for sunset and stargazing
Akaka Falls, Rainbow Falls & Kaumana Caves (Big Island)
Punalu'u black-sand & Papakolea green-sand beaches
Nighttime manta-ray snorkel off Kona
Pu'uhonua o Honaunau & Two Step snorkeling
Haleakala National Park sunrise (Maui)
Road to Hana — Ho'okipa Beach, Twin Falls
Nakalele Blowhole & Black Rock Beach snorkeling (Maui)
Molokini Crater snorkel tour from Maalaea Harbor
tips: Book Haleakala sunrise reservations and the Molokini snorkel tour in advance.
Expect rain and cooler temperatures in winter; pack a light layer.
Allow a full day to fly and transfer between the Big Island and Maui.
The Shaka Guide app has helpful self-guided Big Island routes.

title: Lassen Volcanic National Park
summary: A summer car-camping weekend at Lassen Volcanic National Park, basing at Summit Lake and mixing easy lakeside loops with a strenuous summit of Lassen Peak.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Arrival & set up camp
itinerary.text: Arrive in the evening and set up camp; an easy hike like the Manzanita Lake loop or Echo Lake trail.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Full day of hiking
itinerary.text: Summit Lassen Peak in the morning (~5 miles, 2,000 ft), then Bumpass Hell in the evening; optional Kings Creek Falls.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Sunrise & departure
itinerary.text: Optional sunrise hike, break camp, and leave by early afternoon.

todo: Lassen Peak — strenuous summit hike (~5 miles, ~2,000 ft)
Bumpass Hell — hydrothermal basin trail (~2.7 miles)
Manzanita Lake loop — easy 2-mile sightseeing loop
Echo Lake trail — ~5 miles, trailhead at the campground
Kings Creek Falls / Bench & Sifford Lakes loop (~5 miles)
Kohm Yah-mah-nee Visitor Center
tips: Campfires are not allowed at the campsite — bring a camp stove and gas.
Mosquitoes are heavy in June/July as snow melts; bring strong bug spray.
No showers at the campground; pack wilderness wipes.
Store all food properly per bear-safety guidance.

title: Lost Coast Trail
summary: A rugged 3-day backpacking trip along the Lost Coast Trail in the King Range, hiking roughly 24 miles point-to-point from Mattole Beach to Black Sands Beach, where tide timing is critical.

itinerary.label: Day 0
itinerary.heading: Drive to trailhead area
itinerary.text: Roughly a 5-hour drive to lodging in Redway, near the trailhead.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Shuttle & Mattole to Cooksie Creek
itinerary.text: Early shuttle to Mattole Beach, then hike ~6.5 miles to Cooksie Creek, passing Punta Gorda Lighthouse.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Cooksie Creek to Big Flat
itinerary.text: Tide-sensitive ~9+ mile hike to Big Flat, up to 7 hours.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Big Flat to Black Sands Beach
itinerary.text: About an 8.5-mile hike out, also tide-sensitive.

todo: Punta Gorda Lighthouse — historic lighthouse along the trail
Big Flat — scenic spot to relax and explore
Beach and bluff hiking along the King Range coastline
stay: Motel in Redway, CA — pre-hike base
Backcountry camping at Cooksie Creek and Big Flat
tips: Carefully plan around tides — some sections are impassable at high tide (use NOAA tide predictions).
A bear canister is required; rent one ahead.
Use a point-to-point shuttle service — book in advance.
Bring reliable water filtration; treat clothing with permethrin for ticks.
Campfires are banned — bring a stove.

title: Glacier National Park
summary: A late-September trip to Glacier National Park based out of Kalispell and Whitefish, packed with alpine day hikes, the Going-to-the-Sun Road, and small-town Montana food and breweries.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Going-to-the-Sun corridor
itinerary.text: Hike Avalanche Lake via the Trail of the Cedars, then St. Mary and Virginia Falls. Stargazing at Logan Pass.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Logan Pass & high country
itinerary.text: Drive the Going-to-the-Sun Road, hike Logan Pass to Hidden Lake, and tackle the Highline trail.

itinerary.label: Flex days
itinerary.heading: More hikes
itinerary.text: Grinnell Glacier and the Highline overlook, Red Rock Falls, Siyeh Pass, Swiftcurrent Lake, and Two Medicine Lake.

food: Swift Creek Cafe (Whitefish) — breakfast; eggs Benedict and the Reuben
todo: Going-to-the-Sun Road — the park's iconic scenic drive
Logan Pass — alpine pass; great for stargazing
Avalanche Lake via Trail of the Cedars — short, flat, popular
Hidden Lake from Logan Pass — classic short hike
Grinnell Glacier via the Highline Trail
St. Mary and Virginia Falls — waterfall hike
Lake McDonald, Swiftcurrent & Two Medicine Lakes
Siyeh Pass — long hike starting at Siyeh Bend
stay: Whitefish — cabin-style rentals near dining and breweries
Kalispell — central base near the airport and the park
tips: Carry bear spray on all hikes.
Check the NPS site for trail status and seasonal closures.
Fly into Glacier Park International (FCA) in Kalispell and rent a car.
Late September is cold — pack thermals, warm jackets, and gloves.

title: Bend, Oregon
summary: A summer long-weekend in Bend mixing river floats and lava-tube caves with downtown coffee, food carts, and a lively cocktail-bar scene.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Arrival & downtown
itinerary.text: Explore downtown Bend and the Old Mill District, then an evening Moonlight Canoe Tour with Wanderlust Tours.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Newberry & river float
itinerary.text: Newberry National Volcanic Monument (Lava River Cave, Big Obsidian Flow, Paulina Peak), then a Deschutes River float from Riverbend Park to Drake Park.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Sauna & falls
itinerary.text: A sauna session, brunch downtown, then a hike near Tumalo Falls or Smith Rock before flying out of Redmond.

food: The Sparrow Bakery — the famous Ocean Roll
Wild Rose Northern Thai Eats — dinner
Dogwood at The Pine Shed — craft cocktails
todo: Moonlight Canoe Tour with Wanderlust Tours
Deschutes River float — Riverbend Park to Drake Park
Newberry National Volcanic Monument — Lava River Cave (headlamp required), Big Obsidian Flow, Paulina Peak
Tumalo Falls hikes — 2 to 6.9-mile options
Smith Rock State Park — Misery Ridge Trail (~30 min from Bend)
Soaking pool at McMenamins Old St. Francis School
Old Mill District and downtown Bend
stay: Bend (NW / downtown-adjacent) — central to downtown, the Old Mill District, and the river
tips: Lava River Cave requires a vehicle reservation and a headlamp.
Reserve river-float tube rentals up to 24 hrs ahead.
Fly in/out of Redmond (RDM), about 30 min from Bend.
Newberry parking is covered by a national park pass.

title: Peru
summary: A two-week Peru trip combining Cusco and the Sacred Valley, the multi-day Salkantay trek to Machu Picchu, and time in Lima's food and coastal scene.

itinerary.label: Days 1-2
itinerary.heading: Arrive via Lima to Cusco
itinerary.text: Fly into Lima (grab a SIM and soles), then on to Cusco. Walk around town and book Sacred Valley tours.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Cusco — festival & food
itinerary.text: Catch the Inti Raymi festival and do a food tour around Cusco.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Rainbow Mountain
itinerary.text: Day tour to Rainbow Mountain (Vinicunca).

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Sacred Valley
itinerary.text: Moray, the Maras salt mines, Chinchero, Pisac, and Ollantaytambo.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Salkantay Trek begins
itinerary.text: Drive to Soraypampa, hike up to Humantay Lake, camp at Quiswarniyoc.

itinerary.label: Day 7
itinerary.heading: Salkantay Pass
itinerary.text: Early start over the pass, then a long descent. Camp at Chaullay.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.heading: Jungle & hot springs
itinerary.text: Hike to La Playa Sahuayaco and soak in the Santa Teresa hot springs.

itinerary.label: Day 9
itinerary.heading: Llactapata to Aguas Calientes
itinerary.text: Hike past the Llactapata ruins, ending at a hotel in Aguas Calientes.

itinerary.label: Day 10
itinerary.heading: Machu Picchu
itinerary.text: Pre-dawn start (option to add Huayna Picchu), then evening train back to Cusco.

itinerary.label: Days 11-14
itinerary.heading: Lima
itinerary.text: Fly to Lima, stay in Barranco. Optional Huacachina day trip, a walking tour, and dining before flying home.

food: Astrid y Gastón (Lima) — acclaimed fine dining
todo: Salkantay Trek to Machu Picchu — multi-day high-altitude trek
Machu Picchu — with optional Huayna Picchu add-on
Inti Raymi festival in Cusco (late June)
Rainbow Mountain (Vinicunca) day tour
Sacred Valley — Moray, Maras salt mines, Chinchero, Pisac, Ollantaytambo
Humantay Lake & Santa Teresa hot springs
Cusco food tour
Huacachina desert oasis day trip from Lima
stay: Hostel in the Barranco district, Lima
Camps along the Salkantay trek (Quiswarniyoc, Chaullay, Santa Teresa)
Hotel in Aguas Calientes — base for Machu Picchu
tips: Buy a SIM and exchange soles in central Lima for better rates.
Acclimatize in Cusco before the trek; nights on the trail drop below freezing.
Bring small soles in cash for trail fees and showers.
Book Machu Picchu and trek reservations well in advance.

title: Pacific Northwest Parks Road Trip
summary: A campervan loop from San Francisco through four national parks — Redwoods, Crater Lake, Mt. Rainier, and Olympic — ending with a night in Portland before the long drive home.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: SF to Redwoods
itinerary.text: Pick up the van and drive ~340 miles north. Evening hike (Lady Bird Johnson Grove or Trillium Falls). Camp at Mill Creek Campground.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Redwoods to Crater Lake
itinerary.text: Morning hike (Fern Canyon or Boy Scout Tree Trail) before driving ~214 miles toward Crater Lake.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Crater Lake to Mt. Rainier
itinerary.text: Sunrise hike up Garfield Peak, then a long drive ~387 miles north.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Mt. Rainier
itinerary.text: Hike the Paradise region — Skyline Loop, Golden Gate Trail, or Comet Falls.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Rainier to Olympic
itinerary.text: Drive ~117 miles to Olympic NP. Camp at Sol Duc Hot Springs; campfire and s'mores.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Olympic National Park
itinerary.text: Hikes near Sol Duc — Marymere Falls, Sol Duc Falls.

itinerary.label: Day 7
itinerary.heading: Coast & rainforest
itinerary.text: Ozette Triangle Loop or the Hoh Rainforest Hall of Mosses, Mt. Storm King, or Shi Shi Beach.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.heading: Olympic to Portland
itinerary.text: Drive ~162 miles to Portland; chase waterfalls and a night out in the city.

itinerary.label: Day 9
itinerary.heading: Portland to SF
itinerary.text: Long ~636-mile drive back home.

food: Crater Lake Lodge — breakfast or lunch with a view
todo: Redwoods NP — Avenue of the Giants, James Irvine Trail, Fern Canyon
Crater Lake NP — Garfield Peak sunrise hike, Watchman Peak, Discovery Point
Mt. Rainier NP — Skyline Loop, Golden Gate Trail, Comet Falls
Olympic NP — Marymere & Sol Duc Falls, Hoh Rainforest, Mt. Storm King, Shi Shi Beach
Sol Duc Hot Springs soak
Waterfall hunting around Portland
stay: Mill Creek Campground, Del Norte Coast Redwood SP
Waterwheel RV Park, Chiloquin, OR (Crater Lake)
Sol Duc Hot Springs Resort RV sites (Olympic)
tips: A campervan gets ~13-15 mpg; budget for frequent fill-ups on the long legs.
Some trails (e.g. Tall Trees Grove) need permits booked ahead.
Shi Shi Beach requires a Makah Recreation Pass.
Check road closures in advance.

title: Sikkim, India
summary: A spring trip through the Indian Himalayan state of Sikkim, pairing Gangtok's monasteries and momos with high-altitude lakes and valleys in North Sikkim.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Arrive in Gangtok
itinerary.text: Fly into Bagdogra and transfer to Gangtok (~3 hrs). Visit Rumtek Monastery.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Gangtok highlights
itinerary.text: Day trip to Nathula Pass and Tsomgo (Changu) Lake, then explore MG Road.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Drive to Lachen
itinerary.text: Long drive to Lachen, visiting Gurudongmar Lake, Chopta Valley, and Kala Patthar.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Lachen to Lachung
itinerary.text: Yumthang Valley and Zero Point en route, with stops like Bhim Nala Falls and a hot spring.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Back to Gangtok
itinerary.text: Drive Lachung to Gangtok.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Departure
itinerary.text: Drive from Gangtok back to Bagdogra (~5 hrs) for departure.

food: Taste of Tibet (MG Road, Gangtok) — momos and thukpa
Momo Roll Corner (MG Road, Gangtok) — momos
todo: Rumtek Monastery — major monastery near Gangtok
Nathula Pass — high-altitude border pass
Tsomgo (Changu) Lake — glacial lake near Gangtok
Gurudongmar Lake — one of the highest lakes in the world
Yumthang Valley and Zero Point — valley of flowers and snow point
Seven Sisters Waterfalls and Singhik viewpoint
MG Road, Gangtok — pedestrian market street
tips: Fly into Bagdogra; travel within Sikkim is by shared taxi or private transfer.
North Sikkim spots like Gurudongmar and Nathula require permits via local operators.
Prepare for 5–20°C temps and rain; carry motion-sickness medicine for the winding drives.
Pack layers and acclimatize for the high-altitude spots.

title: St. Lucia
summary: A relaxed Caribbean getaway based in Soufrière — beach and snorkeling days, scenic nature trails with Piton views, sulphur-springs mud baths, and a tree-to-bar chocolate experience.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Arrival & resort
itinerary.text: Transfer to the resort in Soufrière. Settle in, relax by the pool, dinner with Piton views.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Sugar Beach & falls
itinerary.text: Morning yoga, then Sugar Beach for snorkeling (day pass), and an afternoon at Piton (Warm Mineral) Falls.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Tet Paul & Anse Chastanet
itinerary.text: Early Tet Paul Nature Trail and Diamond Falls gardens, then a water taxi to Anse Chastanet for snorkeling and a walk to the quieter Anse Mamin.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Chocolate & Sulphur Springs
itinerary.text: A wood-working workshop, a Hotel Chocolat tree-to-bar experience, and the Sulphur Springs mud baths (go late to miss the crowds).

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Departure
itinerary.text: Breakfast and a final morning relaxing before the afternoon departure.

food: Dasheene — on-site fine dining with Piton views
Treetop — amazing food (the access road is dark at night)
Alor — great local food with good vegetarian options
todo: Sulphur Springs mud baths — the highlight; go late afternoon
Tet Paul Nature Trail — easy, scenic Piton viewpoints
Snorkeling at Sugar Beach and Anse Chastanet Reef
Anse Mamin — quiet, wild jungle beach near Anse Chastanet
Hotel Chocolat tree-to-bar tour
Wood-carving workshop — souvenir-making
stay: Soufrière — open-wall suites with Piton views and complimentary beach shuttles
tips: USD is accepted everywhere.
Dinners are smart casual: collared shirts, trousers, sundresses; no bathing suits.
Book water taxis at the Soufrière dock rather than through hotels for cheaper rates.

title: Utah & Yellowstone Road Trip
summary: A nine-day autumn road trip from California through Yellowstone and Grand Teton, then down to Utah's red-rock parks — Arches, Canyonlands, and Bryce — finishing at the Grand Canyon.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Drive to Elko
itinerary.text: Pick up a rental car and drive from California to Elko, Nevada (~8 hours).

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Salt Flats to Yellowstone
itinerary.text: Morning stop at the Bonneville Salt Flats, then drive to Madison Campground in Yellowstone.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Yellowstone
itinerary.text: Grand Prismatic Hot Spring, West Thumb Geyser Basin, Old Faithful, the Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone, Mammoth Hot Springs, and a dip at Boiling River.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: More Yellowstone & Grand Teton
itinerary.text: Hayden Valley and the Norris Back Basin trail, then into Grand Teton National Park.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Drive to Salt Lake City
itinerary.text: Sunrise, then drive to SLC. Camp at Antelope Island State Park.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Arches
itinerary.text: Drive to Arches National Park, arriving by lunchtime to explore the arches.

itinerary.label: Day 7
itinerary.heading: Canyonlands to Bryce
itinerary.text: Canyonlands, Monument Valley, and Horseshoe Bend en route to Bryce Canyon.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.heading: Bryce Canyon
itinerary.text: A full day exploring Bryce Canyon National Park.

itinerary.label: Day 9
itinerary.heading: Grand Canyon & return
itinerary.text: Visit the Grand Canyon before driving back to the Bay Area.

todo: Grand Prismatic Hot Spring — Fairy Falls trail adds the overlook
Old Faithful & Upper Geyser Basin to Morning Glory Pool
Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone — Artist Point or Uncle Tom's Trail
Mammoth Hot Springs & West Thumb Geyser Basin
Boiling River soak & Hayden Valley wildlife
Bonneville Salt Flats
Arches, Canyonlands, Bryce Canyon & Grand Canyon
Monument Valley and Horseshoe Bend
stay: Antelope Island State Park (Salt Lake City)
tips: Mid-to-late October means cold nights (often below freezing); pack accordingly.
Check Yellowstone road closures before visiting.
Reserve campgrounds and lodging well in advance.

title: Colombia
summary: A nine-day loop through Colombia — colonial Cartagena, the jungle hill town of Minca, vibrant Medellín with Guatapé, and the capital Bogotá — mixing walking tours, waterfall hikes, and food experiences.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Travel day
itinerary.text: Overnight flight to Colombia.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Cartagena
itinerary.text: Afternoon walking tour of the walled old city; ask the guide for dinner recommendations.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: To Minca
itinerary.text: Bus to Santa Marta (~5 hrs), then taxi to Minca. Half-day hike to the 360 Mirador sunset viewpoint.

itinerary.label: Day 4
itinerary.heading: Minca hikes
itinerary.text: Cascadas de Marinka and the Finca la Candelaria chocolate & coffee farm; optional Los Pinos trek past Pozo Azul.

itinerary.label: Day 5
itinerary.heading: Back to Cartagena
itinerary.text: Explore Convento de la Popa, San Felipe de Barajas Fort, and the walled old town.

itinerary.label: Day 6
itinerary.heading: Medellín
itinerary.text: Comuna 13 graffiti tour with street food, then a chef's tasting menu at Carmen.

itinerary.label: Day 7
itinerary.heading: Guatapé
itinerary.text: Full-day Guatapé tour climbing Piedra del Peñol.

itinerary.label: Day 8
itinerary.heading: Bogotá
itinerary.text: Cable car up Monserrate, a food walking tour, Paloquemao market, and La Candelaria.

itinerary.label: Day 9
itinerary.heading: Departure
itinerary.text: Late-night flight home.

food: Carmen (Medellín) — chef's tasting menu with pairings
Amora Cocina Saludable (Minca) — good lunch spot
Bogotá food walking tour & Paloquemao market
todo: Cartagena — walking tour of the walled old town, San Felipe de Barajas Fort
Minca — 360 Mirador sunset hike, Cascadas de Marinka, Pozo Azul
Finca la Candelaria — chocolate & coffee farm tour
Medellín — Comuna 13 graffiti tour, Guatapé and Piedra del Peñol
Bogotá — Monserrate cable car, La Candelaria, Villa de Leyva salt cathedral
tips: Bring cash before heading to Minca; bus tickets are cash-only.
Aim for a bus that drops at the Santa Marta Transportation Center — closer to Minca.
In Bogotá you can leave luggage in airport lockers.
Pack rain gear, mosquito protection, and a headlamp for the jungle hikes.

title: Las Vegas & Page, Arizona
summary: A quick desert getaway pairing the slot canyons and viewpoints around Page, Arizona with the shows, attractions, and dining of the Las Vegas Strip.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Drive to Page
itinerary.text: Fly into Las Vegas and drive ~4.5 hours to Page. Stop at Horseshoe Bend, visit Lake Powell, and stargaze.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Antelope Canyon & back to Vegas
itinerary.text: Lower and Upper Antelope Canyon tours, then drive back to Las Vegas. Walk the Strip and catch a show.

itinerary.label: Day 3
itinerary.heading: Vegas highlights & departure
itinerary.text: Brunch, the Bellagio fountains, the Sphere, Omega Mart, and the Ethel M cactus garden before the evening flight home.

todo: Horseshoe Bend (Page)
Lower and Upper Antelope Canyon guided tours (Page)
Toadstool Hoodoos & Glen Canyon Dam overlook
Lake Powell — kayaking
Omega Mart (Area15)
Bellagio fountains and conservatory
The Sphere
High Roller observation wheel at sunset
Penn & Teller and Absinthe shows
tips: Book Antelope Canyon tours in advance; lower and upper are separate tours.
Allow ~4.5 hours each way between Las Vegas and Page.
Be aware of Las Vegas resort fees on top of room rates.

title: Sonoma & Ukiah Wine Country
summary: A cozy rainy-weekend road trip from San Francisco through the Sonoma redwoods and wineries up to Ukiah, mixing misty nature stops, wine tasting, and vegetarian dining.

itinerary.label: Day 1
itinerary.heading: Redwoods, wine & Ukiah
itinerary.text: Drive north on US-101. Armstrong Redwoods State Natural Reserve, lunch in Healdsburg, wine tasting at Ridge Vineyards, then dinner in Ukiah.

itinerary.label: Day 2
itinerary.heading: Ukiah sights & scenic return
itinerary.text: Breakfast and yoga, then the City of Ten Thousand Buddhas before the scenic drive back to San Francisco.

food: Cultivo (Ukiah) — vegetarian; tacos, enchiladas, veggie bowls
Jyun Kang Vegetarian Restaurant — dumplings and noodle soups
Dry Creek General Store — deli sandwiches
todo: Armstrong Redwoods State Natural Reserve — Pioneer Nature Trail
Ridge Vineyards — Zinfandel and Cabernet tasting
Mill Creek Vineyards — BYO-picnic tasting
City of Ten Thousand Buddhas — Buddha Hall and gardens
Optional stroll near Lake Mendocino
tips: A great rainy-weekend option — tasting rooms and the temple work indoors.
Ukiah is about 40 minutes north of the Healdsburg wineries.
